Zimbabwe.headline: Zimbabwe Deports 28 Mozambican Teens, Visually Impaired Man Engaged in Street Vending and Begging
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe has deported twenty-nine undocumented Mozambican nationals, including 28 boys aged between 14 and 18 and one visually impaired man, after they were found to be in the country illegally and engaged in informal street vending and begging. The deported Mozambicans were received at the Machipanda Border Post by a Mozambican multi-sectoral team.

Zimbabwe.headline: VP Chiwenga shares leadership principles with students abroad
Zimbabwe.detail: Vice President Chiwenga has been sharing leadership principles with students abroad. The Vice President's efforts highlight the importance of fostering strong leadership qualities among the next generation of Zimbabweans, even those studying outside the country.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mash West boost winter wheat production
Zimbabwe.detail: Mashonaland West is working to boost winter wheat production. This initiative aims to enhance the region's agricultural output and contribute to national food security.
President Mnangagwa is urging the youth to uphold their vows. The President's message underscores the importance of young people staying true to their commitments and values.
Zimbabwe.headline: President implores youth to uphold vows

Zimbabwe.detail: SADC is taking steps to address a 1.6 million tonne fish gap. This initiative aims to boost fish production and ensure food security within the Southern African Development Community.
Zimbabwe.headline: SADC moves to plug 1,6m tonne fish gap

Education.headline: UK universities warn of cuts for impoverished students if dire funding issues continue
Education.detail: UK universities are warning of potential cuts affecting impoverished students due to ongoing funding problems.

Education.headline: Harvard faculty vote to limit A grades for undergraduates
Education.detail: Harvard faculty voted to limit the number of A grades undergraduates can receive in an effort to combat grade inflation.
The White House budget office is withholding over $2 billion in congressionally approved funds from U.S. Department of Education accounts.
Education.headline: White House Blocks $2 Billion for Education: See All the Affected Programs
Facts & Stories with the 2026 NEA Higher Educator of the Year
Education.detail: Clinton Smith, a Tennessee professor of special education, has been named this year's NEA Higher Educator of the Year.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education Issues Final Rule to Create New Workforce Pell Grant Program
Education.detail: The U.S. Department of Education has announced a final rule to implement the Workforce Pell Grant program, which was created under President Trump's Working Families Tax Cuts Act.

Politics.detail: President Trump is facing scrutiny regarding his health as he prepares for another visit to Walter Reed, with independent physicians questioning the White House's lack of transparency on key health matters.
Politics.headline: Trump faces health questions ahead of another Walter Reed trip

Politics.detail: The U.S. and Iran are reportedly working towards an agreement to extend a ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, leading to a fall in oil prices and a rise in global stocks.
Politics.headline: U.S. and Iran work toward deal to extend ceasefire and reopen Strait of Hormuz

Politics.detail: Chinese President Xi Jinping has lauded the 'unbreakable' ties between China and Pakistan during a visit by Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif to Beijing, reinforcing the strong relationship between the two countries.
Politics.headline: China's Xi praises 'unbreakable' Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing

Politics.detail: Three current state lawmakers are competing for seats on California's Board of Equalization, which was stripped of power in 2017 but remains a launching pad for statewide political office.
Politics.headline: California's Board of Equalization is a coveted spot once again for state lawmakers looking for a new gig
Federal Government is launching a campaign to help women going through menopause and perimenopause
Politics.detail: The Australian Federal Government is launching a campaign to help women going through menopause and perimenopause to better understand their symptoms.

Technology.detail: A ZDNET writer tested the 100x zoom capabilities of three flagship phones to determine which performed best.
Technology.headline: I tested 100x zoom on the Galaxy S26 Ultra, Pixel 10 Pro, and Razr Fold - here's who won

Technology.detail: A ZDNET writer reviews the new features and updates coming to Android Auto.
Technology.headline: I saw the future of Android Auto, and now Google has me dreading my own car
Final frontier for meds? UK startup sends drug-making into space
Technology.detail: A UK startup is sending drug-making into space.

Technology.headline: Mathematicians solve decades-old mystery about the hidden order in high-dimensional randomness
Technology.detail: Mathematicians have solved a decades-old mystery concerning the hidden order within high-dimensional randomness.
Palantir is disputing Sadiq Khan's decision to block a £50m contract with the Met Police.
Technology.headline: Palantir hits back at Sadiq Khan after £50m contract with Met police blocked

Entertainment.detail: OnlyFans model Lily Phillips will be a bridesmaid for Annie Knight, who is marrying Henry Brayshaw in Australia in October, as announced in an emotional Instagram video. Many stars have grown their families in 2026, including Charles Melton, Carly Rae Jepsen, Charlie Puth and Hailee Steinfeld.
Entertainment.headline: Lily Phillips to Serve as Bridesmaid for OnlyFans Model Annie Knight

Entertainment.detail: Grammy-nominated rapper and singer-songwriter 6LACK sat down with AP Entertainment journalist Leslie Ambriz to discuss his evolution as an artist, finding his purpose, mental health and encouraging fans to “heal from their trauma”. Now a 33-year-old father, the artist says that family is cool and being vulnerable is cool.
Entertainment.headline: 6LACK Says ‘Love is the New Gangsta’ Represents His Most Personal Album
What Is Stephen Colbert Doing Next After 'The Late Show'?
Entertainment.detail: CBS’s cancellation of ‘The Late Show’ has become one of the most controversial decisions in entertainment history. This article discusses what Stephen Colbert is doing now after the show's cancellation.

Entertainment.headline: Vijay had 'narrow escape from death' during shoot atop moving train over 2,500-ft valley
Entertainment.detail: Actor Vijay had a narrow escape from death while shooting on top of a moving train over a 2,500-foot valley. The incident was reported on May 25, 2026.

Entertainment.headline: The movies to tune in for this summer
Entertainment.detail: Disney's latest addition to the Star Wars franchise, "The Mandalorian and Grogu," is kicking off the summer slate of blockbusters. Some other highly-anticipated movies that will be tested at the box office this summer are Steven Spielberg's "Disclosure Day," "Toy Story 5" and "Supergirl".

Finance & Economy.detail: Brian Roberts has resigned as the CEO of RMH, a South African investment holding company.
Finance & Economy.headline: RMH CEO Brian Roberts resigns
BHP cuts green push across iron ore segment, media report says
Finance & Economy.detail: BHP has reportedly scaled back its green initiatives within its iron ore operations, according to media reports.

Finance & Economy.headline: No further fuel levy relief, as Treasury rebalances books
Finance & Economy.detail: South Africa's Treasury will not provide additional fuel levy relief as it works to stabilize the country's finances.

Finance & Economy.headline: Sita denies Nigerian 'hactivists' gained access to SA government systems
Finance & Economy.detail: Sita, the State Information Technology Agency of South Africa, has refuted claims that Nigerian 'hactivists' breached its systems.

Finance & Economy.headline: PSU closure: Govt sets in motion new move after years wasted in stop-start process
Finance & Economy.detail: The Indian government is initiating a renewed effort to close down Public Sector Undertakings (PSUs) after previous attempts faced setbacks.

Lifestyle.headline: The D.C. concierge who's spent half a century catering to guests' every whim
Lifestyle.detail: Robert Watson, one of Washington's most well-connected and beloved fixtures, knows how to nab reservations, hot tickets and the perfect blowout. Watson has been catering to guests for half a century.

Lifestyle.headline: Kiln Expands Into North Texas With Lifestyle-Focused Coworking Offices
Lifestyle.detail: Coworking brand Kiln is expanding into North Texas with new locations, offering hospitality-driven office spaces. Kiln focuses on community, flexibility, and workplace experience.

CJ Group Chairman Jay Lee visited the United States to review the group’s potential to expand its K-lifestyle platform, emphasizing the need to enhance global competitiveness. The CJ Cup should be expanded beyond a golf tournament into a platform where people can directly experience and enjoy K-lifestyle in the U.S.
Lifestyle.headline: CJ Chairman Lee Visits The CJ Cup in U.S., Vows K-Lifestyle Platform Expansion

Lifestyle.detail: More Malaysians are now eligible for free flu vaccines, which is especially important for year-round protection due to the constant circulation of influenza in Malaysia. Aging weakens the immune system, making vaccinations crucial for maintaining strength and overall well-being.
Lifestyle.headline: More Malaysians can get a free flu vaccine
From post-workout hygiene to better sleep habits, these 5 wellness rituals are shaping women's self-care in 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Women in India are focusing on targeted and consistent personal care routines, built on a genuine understanding of how the body works. This includes post-exercise hygiene, sleep-time hygiene, and skin barrier maintenance.

Sports.headline: Seasoned sports chief to lead Tennis Australia
Sports.detail: NRL CEO Andrew Abdo has been appointed the new Tennis Australia Chief Executive Officer, succeeding Craig Tiley, who will depart to lead the USTA.

Sports.headline: Wembanyama answers the call again, Spurs top Thunder to tie West finals at 2-2
Sports.detail: Victor Wembanyama led the San Antonio Spurs to victory over the Oklahoma City Thunder, leveling the Western Conference finals series.

Sports.headline: Enhanced Games claim 'we changed the world' but only one record broken and three clean athletes win
Sports.detail: The Enhanced Games, which allowed performance enhancements, saw only one record broken and three clean athletes win, leading to some criticism.

Sports.headline: Mercedes' teen star Antonelli claims Canadian GP for 4th consecutive F1 win after Russell exits
Sports.detail: Kimi Antonelli of Mercedes won the Canadian Grand Prix, marking his fourth consecutive F1 victory after his teammate George Russell had to exit the race due to engine failure.

Sports.headline: AFL Off-Season Central: Recent recruit retires mid-year in selfless timing to help club's draft plans
Sports.detail: Shane McAdam retired from the Melbourne Demons to return home to WA, allowing the club to open up a third mid-season draft pick.

Crime & Justice.detail: A West Australian man who imported cocaine into Perth from Ireland and Northern Ireland has been sentenced to 10 years' imprisonment after concealing the drugs in face masks, curry packets, and tomato soup sachets.
Crime & Justice.headline: WA man jailed for importing cocaine in soup and curry sauce sachets

Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice is acknowledging it has removed from its website news releases about criminal cases related to the Jan. 6, 2021 riot, calling the information about the prosecutions "partisan propaganda."
Crime & Justice.headline: DOJ says it scrubbed news releases about Jan. 6 criminal cases from its website

Crime & Justice.detail: The Democratic Alliance (DA) has uncovered indications that the South African Police Service (SAPS) in the Western Cape have allowed members convicted of criminal offenses to remain in service without properly implementing Regulation 5(3)(dd) of the SAPS Discipline.
Crime & Justice.headline: DA lays complaint after 164 criminal convictions linked to WC SAPS members
Police hypnotized a witness. Now a Texas man faces death.
Crime & Justice.detail: Charles Flores sits on Texas's death row based on unreliable testimony by a witness who had been hypnotized by law enforcement, with the only evidence placing Flores at the scene of the 1998 murder being the in-court identification by this witness.

Crime & Justice.headline: Bystander in serious condition after fatal shooting near White House
Crime & Justice.detail: A bystander was seriously wounded after a 21-year-old man, identified as Nasire Best, opened fire on a security checkpoint outside the White House and was fatally shot by US Secret Service officers; the incident prompted President Trump to request $1 billion for security upgrades.

Science.headline: Scientists Uncover Twist in How Cells Behave When Division Goes Wrong
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered a surprising twist in how cells behave when division goes wrong; sometimes a cell successfully copies its DNA but fails to split into two, leaving it with double the genetic material, a mistake linked to aging and cancer.
A mysterious little blue octopus discovered nearly 6,000 feet beneath the waters of the Galápagos Islands has officially been identified as a brand-new species; it is about the size of a golf ball.
Science.headline: Mysterious Little Blue Octopus Identified as New Species
Newly Identified Brain Protein May Play a Major Role in How the Body Ages
Science.detail: Researchers discovered that declining levels of Menin in the hypothalamus triggered inflammation, memory problems, bone loss, and other aging-related changes in mice; restoring Menin reversed several of these effects.

Scientists have created an AI-powered system that can scan and map an entire mouse body in extraordinary detail, revealing that obesity appears to damage facial sensory nerves and trigger widespread inflammation.
Science.headline: AI System Maps Mouse Body and Uncovers Obesity Effects

Science.detail: A new nightly sleep apnea pill has been fast-tracked for approval following a successful trial.
Science.headline: Nightly Sleep Apnea Pill Fast-Tracked For Approval After Latest Trial Success

Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses how some wealthy countries have more robust safety nets than America, financed by broad levies on the middle class, and as rising defense spending squeezes national budgets, those already high taxes are rising. The tax wedge for a single worker reached its highest level in a decade.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: How big would your tax burden be in another rich country?

Opinion/Editorial.detail: Max Boot argues that Netanyahu's desire to lash out in all directions after a devastating sneak attack is backfiring, overextending Israel's military, turning the state into a global pariah, and making Israel more dependent than ever on U.S. protection even as his country's unpopularity in America rises. In pursuit of absolute security, he is undermining Israel's long-term interests.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trying to remake the Middle East, Netanyahu damaged Israel's security
22,581 days of the Voting Rights Act
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses the importance of the Black vote and the ongoing struggle for voting rights, noting that oppression adapts and Connecticut is not exempt from that history, as Black communities remain underfunded, underrepresented, and too often ignored in the state. The author emphasizes the need to continue organizing, investing, and acting to prioritize democracy.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Bracing for a super El Niño
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This editorial discusses preparing for a super El Niño event.

An editorial highlights that Kenya's taxation debate has become a tired annual ritual, with promises made every budget cycle about widening the tax bracket, yet the burden continues to fall disproportionately on salaried workers. The government must move beyond rhetoric and confront the structural imbalance that has overtaxed formal employees while leaving large sections of the economy outside the tax net.
Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Tax reform must go beyond rhetoric

Health.detail: Researchers found that adding bananas to berry smoothies can dramatically reduce the body's ability to absorb healthy flavanols, showing that even simple food combinations can change how much nutrition your body actually gets.
Health.headline: Surprising Research Reveals Why You Shouldn't Add Bananas to Your Smoothies
Scientists “Recharge” Damaged Nerves to Ease Chronic Pain
Health.detail: Scientists may have uncovered a radically new way to stop chronic nerve pain at the source by reviving damaged nerves.

Health.headline: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
Health.detail: As of Sunday, May 24, 2026, a measles outbreak in Bangladesh has resulted in over 500 deaths, mostly children, raising concerns about the country's response.
Africa is racing to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak threatening 10 countries as infections spill from eastern Congo into Uganda.
Health.headline: DR Congo Ebola cases rise amid distrust, armed conflict zone
Woodchips on trails reduce tick populations, Ottawa researchers find
Health.detail: A study published this month by researchers at the University of Ottawa has found that applying woodchips to some trails significantly reduced local tick populations.

World.detail: President Trump and other administration officials are tempering expectations raised of an imminent agreement to end the war in Iran, while Iranian officials have signaled there are still disagreements on key issues.
World.headline: U.S.-Iran peace deal emerging, while war threats still loom

World.detail: The WHO chief says there have been 220 suspected deaths in the ongoing Ebola outbreak as Ugandan health authorities report two new Ebola infections.
World.headline: Ebola death toll climbs in Congo as new infections confirmed in Uganda
China launches Shenzhou 23 spacecraft with 1 of 3 astronauts set for yearlong stay
World.detail: China has launched its Shenzhou 23 spacecraft, carrying the first of three astronauts who will stay in space for a year.

World.headline: Trump says not to rush as details emerge of a potential Iran deal
World.detail: Details are emerging about a potential deal to end the Iran war, but President Trump suggests there's no need to rush.
Qatar has sent mediators to Tehran, signaling that talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz are reaching a climax.
World.headline: Qatar sends mediators to Tehran in sign talks to reopen strait of Hormuz are reaching climax
